content:
# Numbers Chapter 28 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pp. 182–183 | **Source Image:** `NUM_028_page_001.jpg`, `NUM_028_page_002.jpg`
**Secondary Source:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. pa_0135–pa_0136

---

**1.** And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying:

**2.** "Command the sons of Israel and say to them: 'My offering, my food for my fire-offerings, my pleasing aroma, you shall be careful to offer to me at its appointed time.'

**3.** And you shall say to them: 'This [is] the fire-offering which you shall offer to YHWH: male lambs, sons of a year, unblemished, two for a day, [as] a continual burnt offering.

**4.** The one lamb you shall offer in the morning, and the second lamb you shall offer between the two evenings,

**5.** and a tenth of an ephah of fine flour for a grain offering, mixed with beaten oil, a fourth of a hin.

**6.** [It is] a continual burnt offering which was ordained at Mount Sinai, for a pleasing aroma, a fire-offering to YHWH.

**7.** And its drink offering: a fourth of a hin for the one lamb. In the holy [place] you shall pour out a drink offering of strong drink to YHWH.

**8.** And the second lamb you shall offer between the two evenings; like the grain offering of the morning and like its drink offering you shall offer [it], a fire-offering of a pleasing aroma to YHWH.

**9.** And on the day of the Sabbath: two male lambs, sons of a year, unblemished, and two-tenths [of an ephah] of fine flour [for] a grain offering, mixed with oil, and its drink offering —

**10.** [this is] the burnt offering of the Sabbath on its Sabbath, in addition to the continual burnt offering and its drink offering.

**11.** And at the beginnings of your months you shall offer a burnt offering to YHWH: two young bulls, sons of the herd, and one ram, male lambs, sons of a year, seven, unblemished;

**12.** and three-tenths [of an ephah] of fine flour [for] a grain offering, mixed with oil, for the one bull; and two-tenths [of an ephah] of fine flour [for] a grain offering, mixed with oil, for the one ram;

**13.** and a tenth, a tenth [of an ephah] of fine flour [for] a grain offering, mixed with oil, for the one lamb — a burnt offering, a pleasing aroma, a fire-offering to YHWH.

**14.** And their drink offerings: half of a hin shall be for the bull, and a third of a hin for the ram, and a fourth of a hin for the lamb, of wine. This [is] the burnt offering of the month in its month, for the months of the year.

**15.** And one male goat for a sin offering to YHWH; in addition to the continual burnt offering it shall be offered, and its drink offering.

**16.** And in the first month, on the fourteenth day of the month, [is] a Passover to YHWH.

**17.** And on the fifteenth day of this month, a feast; seven days unleavened bread shall be eaten.

**18.** On the first day, a holy convocation; any work of service you shall not do.

**19.** And you shall offer a fire-offering, a burnt offering, to YHWH: two young bulls, sons of the herd, and one ram, and seven male lambs, sons of a year; unblemished they shall be for you.

**20.** And their grain offering: fine flour mixed with oil; three-tenths [of an ephah] for the bull and two-tenths for the ram you shall offer.

**21.** A tenth, a tenth [of an ephah] you shall offer for the one lamb, for the seven lambs;

**22.** and one male goat [for] a sin offering, to make atonement upon you.

**23.** Apart from the burnt offering of the morning which [is] for the continual burnt offering, you shall offer these.

**24.** Like these you shall offer for the day, [for] seven days, food of a fire-offering, a pleasing aroma to YHWH; in addition to the continual burnt offering it shall be offered, and its drink offering.

**25.** And on the seventh day a holy convocation shall be for you; any work of service you shall not do.

**26.** And on the day of the firstfruits, when you offer a new grain offering to YHWH, in your Weeks, a holy convocation shall be for you; any work of service you shall not do.

**27.** And you shall offer a burnt offering for a pleasing aroma to YHWH: two young bulls, sons of the herd, one ram, seven male lambs, sons of a year;

**28.** and their grain offering: fine flour mixed with oil, three-tenths [of an ephah] for the one bull, two-tenths for the one ram,

**29.** a tenth, a tenth [of an ephah] for the one lamb, for the seven lambs;

**30.** one male goat, to make atonement upon you.

**31.** Apart from the continual burnt offering and its grain offering, you shall offer [them] — unblemished they shall be for you — and their drink offerings.'"

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

**General:** Numbers 28 constitutes YHWH's comprehensive restatement of the sacrificial calendar, addressed through Moses to the sons of Israel. The chapter moves from daily (tamid) to Sabbath to new-moon to Passover/Unleavened Bread to Firstfruits offerings. The repetitive formulaic structure is characteristic of priestly legislative material and has been preserved without smoothing.

**Verse 2 — קָרְבָּנִי לַחְמִי לְאִשַּׁי (qorbani lachmi l'ishshai):** The triple possessive "my offering, my food, for my fire-offerings" is preserved literally. The Hebrew לֶחֶם (lechem, "bread/food") here refers to sacrificial food generally, not literal bread. The Vaticanus reads τὰ δῶρά μου δόματά μου καρπώματά μου ("my gifts, my offerings, my fruit-offerings"), expanding the Hebrew slightly.

**Verse 3 — בְּנֵי שָׁנָה (b'nei shanah):** Literally "sons of a year," meaning yearling lambs. This Hebraism is preserved per EMET convention rather than smoothing to "one year old."

**Verse 4 — בֵּין הָעַרְבָּיִם (bein ha'arbayim):** Literally "between the two evenings." This technical term for the late-afternoon sacrificial time (approximately 3:00 PM) is debated — some understand it as between sunset and full darkness, others between the decline of the sun and sunset. The dual form is preserved literally.

**Verse 6 — הָעֲשֻׂיָה (ha'asuyah):** "Which was ordained" — literally "the one-being-made/done." This passive participle refers back to the institution of the tamid offering at Sinai (Exodus 29:38–42). The Vaticanus has ἣν ἐποιήσατε ("which you made/offered"), a second-person rendering that differs from the MT's passive.

**Verse 7 — שֵׁכָר (shekhar):** "Strong drink" — this term is sometimes rendered "fermented drink" or "beer." In this sacrificial context, it refers to the drink offering (nesekh) being poured out. The Vaticanus reads σπονδὴν σικερα ("a libation of sikera"), transliterating the Hebrew term. The instruction to pour it "in the holy [place]" (בַּקֹּדֶשׁ, baqqodesh) specifies the sacred precinct.

**Verse 9 — וּשְׁנֵי עֶשְׂרֹנִים (ush'nei esronim):** "Two-tenths" — the unit (ephah) is implied and placed in brackets.

**Verses 13, 21, 29 — עִשָּׂרוֹן עִשָּׂרוֹן (issaron issaron):** The repetition "a tenth, a tenth" is the distributive construction meaning "a tenth [for] each." This doubling is preserved literally rather than smoothed to "a tenth each."

**Verse 14 — חֹדֶשׁ בְּחָדְשׁוֹ (chodesh b'chodsho):** "Month in its month" — an idiomatic expression meaning "each month" or "month by month." Preserved literally.

**Verse 15 — עַל עֹלַת הַתָּמִיד (al olat hatamid):** "In addition to the continual burnt offering" — the preposition עַל here carries the sense of "on top of" or "in addition to," indicating these monthly offerings are supplementary to the daily tamid.

**Verse 17 — חָג (chag):** Simply "a feast" — the Hebrew does not name the festival explicitly here but identifies it by context as the Feast of Unleavened Bread (Matzot).

**Verse 22 — לְכַפֵּר עֲלֵיכֶם (l'khapper aleikhem):** "To make atonement upon you" — the preposition עַל with the verb כפר is rendered "upon" rather than the more natural English "for," preserving the spatial metaphor of the Hebrew.

**Verse 26 — בְּשָׁבֻעֹתֵיכֶם (b'shavu'oteikhem):** "In your Weeks" — referring to the Festival of Weeks (Shavuot). This is a temporal marker identifying when the firstfruits offering occurs, at the conclusion of the seven-week count from Passover.

**LXX Vaticanus Notes:** The Greek text on pa_0135 and pa_0136 is largely consistent with the MT of Numbers 28. Minor variations include: (1) the Vaticanus occasionally specifies ἀμώμους ("unblemished") where the Hebrew word order differs slightly; (2) the Greek tends to make explicit the unit of measure (δέκατον, "tenth") where the Hebrew relies on context; (3) verse 6 shows the notable variant noted above (active vs. passive voice). No substantive divergences affecting the verse count or major content were observed.

— EMET
